content: Standard ILS capture logic: intercept angle to localizer should be under ~30 degrees; localizer (lateral) must capture before glideslope (vertical) engages; glideslope is normally intercepted from below (aircraft levels off below GS altitude, waits for glideslope needle to center, then begins descent) to avoid false glideslope lobes. Autopilot/simulator implementations track angular deviation from localizer course and glideslope angle, converting to lateral/vertical correction commands once within capture criteria.
